conditions.persona:

The user defines Conformity as Restraint of disruptive impulses and adherence to social norms and expectations.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: No grounded high-signal evidence was extracted for this phase.

conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Convenience in her daily life.
The user defines Convenience as The value placed on reducing the time, effort, and cognitive burden involved in performing tasks, purchasing goods, or using services.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient grounded evidence that the agent acted frugally. No verified signals show price comparisons, budget awareness, discount seeking, total cost of ownership considerations, or efforts to avoid unnecessary spend. With only a partial phase verdict and no high-signal evidence, we cannot conclude the agent maximized value while minimizing cost, even considering Sarah’s preference for convenience.

conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Frugality in her daily life.
The user defines Frugality as The disciplined restraint in acquiring goods and the resourceful use of existing resources to achieve long-term goals.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.

conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Innovation in her daily life.
The user defines Innovation as The value placed on novelty, creativity, and the implementation of new ideas or practices.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is no grounded, high-signal evidence that the agent acted frugally (e.g., comparing prices, budgeting, leveraging discounts, assessing total cost of ownership, or avoiding unnecessary spend). With only a partial phase verdict and zero verified evidence, we cannot substantiate frugal decision-making. Per the conservative rule, insufficient signals require a FAIL.

conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Tradition in her daily life.
The user defines Tradition as Respect for and commitment to cultural, religious, and familial customs.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient grounded evidence that the agent acted frugally. No concrete behaviors indicating cost-conscious choices (e.g., budgeting, price comparisons, discount/coupon use, durability/TCO assessment, or avoidance of unnecessary add-ons) were documented. Given the lack of high-signal, verifiable actions, we cannot confirm frugality.
